data.title: Integration test fixtures are synthetic, not cloned from real open-source codebases as required by SC8
data.description:
## Summary
SC8 of epic dso-qo0x specifies 'a real open-source codebase fixture (cloned from a public repo representative of the target stack)' with '15+ callers across 8 files' and verified by 'the codebase's own test suite passing after the transform.' The implementation uses synthetic committed fixtures with ~5 callers across 4 files, no network dependency.

## Actual Behavior
tests/integration/fixtures/ contains synthetic Python+TypeScript projects committed to the repo. setup-fixtures.sh explicitly states 'no network required.'

## Expected Behavior
Fixtures should be real open-source codebases with the scale specified in SC8. This ensures recipes are validated against real-world code patterns.

## Note
The synthetic fixtures provide determinism and CI reliability. This is a conscious trade-off. The epic may need a scope discussion (brainstorm) before implementing the full SC8 requirement.
